<Meta title="Atoms/Link Skip" />

# Link Skip

The link skip component provides an accessibility-focused navigation aid that allows keyboard users to bypass repetitive navigation and jump directly to the main content of a page. This component is visually hidden by default and only appears when it receives keyboard focus, ensuring compliance with WCAG 2.1 accessibility guidelines.

## Interactive Preview

Use the controls panel below to experiment with the link skip's properties. Press Tab to see the skip link appear at the top of the page.

<div className="sb-contained-canvas">
  <Canvas of={LinkSkipStories.LinkSkip} />
</div>
<Controls of={LinkSkipStories.LinkSkip} />

## Technical Specifications

- **Atomic Level**: Atom
- **Template**: `yds-link-skip.twig`
- **Styles**: `_yds-link-skip.scss`

### Accessibility

The link skip component is a critical accessibility feature that provides several benefits:

- **Keyboard Navigation**: Appears when users navigate with the Tab key
- **Screen Reader Support**: Properly announced by assistive technologies
- **WCAG 2.1 Compliance**: Meets WCAG 2.1 Level A Success Criterion 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks)
- **Visual Feedback**: Clear focus indicators when the link receives keyboard focus
- **Skip Navigation**: Allows users to bypass repetitive content and jump to main content

### Behavior

- **Visually Hidden by Default**: The skip link is positioned off-screen using CSS
- **Appears on Focus**: When the link receives keyboard focus (via Tab), it becomes visible
- **First Interactive Element**: Should be the first focusable element on the page
- **Anchor Navigation**: Clicking or activating the link scrolls to the target anchor
- **No JavaScript Required**: Works entirely with HTML and CSS

## Usage Guidelines

### Best Practices

- Place the skip link as the very first element in your page's HTML
- Use descriptive text like "Skip to main content" or "Skip to content"
- Ensure the target anchor (e.g., `#main-content`) exists on the page
- Apply the skip link to a container element that wraps the main content
- Test with keyboard navigation to ensure proper visibility and functionality
- Use consistent skip link text across all pages of your site

### Things to Avoid

- Don't hide the skip link entirely (it must be available to keyboard users)
- Don't place the skip link after other focusable elements
- Don't use vague text like "Skip" without context
- Don't create multiple skip links pointing to the same target
- Don't forget to add the corresponding ID to your main content area
- Don't use JavaScript-dependent implementations when CSS is sufficient

### When to Use Link Skip

The link skip component should be used on:

- All pages with repeated navigation elements
- Pages with complex headers or navigation menus
- Sites with multiple navigation regions
- Any page where users would benefit from bypassing repetitive content

### Implementation Example

```html
<!-- Skip link (first element in body) -->
<a href="#main-content" class="link-skip">Skip to main content</a>

<!-- Site header and navigation -->
<header>...</header>
<nav>...</nav>

<!-- Main content with corresponding ID -->
<main id="main-content">
  <!-- Page content -->
</main>
```

## Testing

For comprehensive visual regression testing with all theme variations, see the [Visreg page](?path=/story/atoms-link-skip-visreg--visreg).

### Testing Skip Link Functionality

When testing the skip link:

1. Load a page with the skip link component
2. Press the Tab key once (skip link should appear at the top)
3. The skip link should have clear visual focus indicators
4. Press Enter or Space to activate the link
5. The page should scroll to the main content area
6. Focus should move to the main content container

### Testing Accessibility

- Use a screen reader to verify the link is properly announced
- Ensure the link appears before any other interactive elements
- Verify sufficient color contrast in the visible state
- Test keyboard navigation flow from skip link to main content

## Related Components

- **Navigation**: Works in conjunction with site navigation to improve accessibility
- **Header**: Typically placed before or within the site header region
